description: 1944 one of the five sectors of the Allied invasion of German-occupied France
description_wiki: Omaha Beach was one of five Allied beach landing sectors of the amphibious assault component of Operation Overlord during the Second World War.

surface_forms: the evening of the fourth of June, 1944
the fortieth anniversary of World War II
description: 1944 landing operations of the Allied invasion of Normandy
description_wiki: The Normandy landings were the landing operations and associated airborne operations on 6 June 1944 of the Allied invasion of Normandy in Operation Overlord during the Second World War.
Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.
Caen (UK: ; French: [kɑ̃] ; Norman: Kaem) is a city and commune 15 km (9.3 mi) inland from the northwestern coast of France.
The Avro Lancaster, commonly known as the Lancaster Bomber, is a British Second World War heavy bomber.

description: arm of the Atlantic Ocean that separates southern England from northern France
description_wiki: The English Channel, also known as the Channel, is an arm of the Atlantic Ocean that separates Southern England from northern France.
The Purple Heart (PH) is a United States military decoration awarded in the name of the president to those wounded or killed while serving on or after 5 April 1917, with the U.S. military.

description: capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom
description_wiki: London is the capital and largest city of England and the United Kingdom, with a population of 9.1 million people in 2024.
A Quonset hut  is a lightweight prefabricated structure of corrugated galvanized steel with a semi-circular cross-section.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.
Brussels, officially the Brussels-Capital Region, is a region of Belgium comprising 19 municipalities, including the City of Brussels, which is the capital of Belgium.

surface_forms: the fortieth anniversary of World War II
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: country located primarily in North America
description_wiki: The United States of America (USA), also known as the United States (U.S.) or America, is a country primarily located in North America.

description: private university in Evanston, Illinois, U.S.
description_wiki: Northwestern University (NU) is a private research university in Evanston, Illinois, United States.

entity: Atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
surface_forms: the atom bombs were dropped on Japan
description: 1945 use of nuclear weapons against Japan in World War II
description_wiki: On 6 and 9 August 1945, the United States detonated two atomic bombs over the Japanese cities of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, respectively, during the final days of World War II. The aerial bombings killed 150,000 to 246,000 people, most of whom were civilians, and remain the first and only uses of nuclear weapons in an armed conflict.
Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: cargo ships built in the United States during World War II
description_wiki: Liberty ships are a class of cargo ship built in the United States during World War II under the Emergency Shipbuilding Program.
